string.text: Nimble sent me. He asked me to tell you to focus surveillance on the movement of mutants and Bandits in Dark Valley. Here, these are special surveillance devices.
Understood. But what are these devices for?
I don't know. He just said to pass them along and that he'll explain later.
Another fancy gadget from the talented folks in Duty, I guess.
Maybe. Well, see you around.
Nimble sent me. He asked me to tell you to focus surveillance on the movement of Stalkers in Cordon. Here, these are special surveillance devices.
Alright. But what are these devices for? Some kind of biolocator?
I don't know. He just said to pass them along and that he'll explain later.
That's just like him. Instead of a good weapon or sturdy armor, he sends this.
Well, take care then.
Nimble sent me. He asked me to tell you to focus surveillance on the movement of Bandits in Agroprom. Here, these are special surveillance devices.
Alright. But what are these devices for? How do we use them on people?
I don't know. He just said to pass them along and that he'll explain later why they're needed.
I see, that's just like him. Instead of providing some gear, he sends this.
Well, take care then.
Nimble sent me. He asked me to tell you to focus surveillance on the movement of Stalkers in the Bar. Here, these are special surveillance devices.
Alright. But what are these devices for? Some kind of biolocator?
I don't know. He just said to pass them along and that he'll explain later why they're needed.
I see, another fancy gadget from Duty I suppose.
Well, take care then.
Nimble sent me. He asked me to tell you to focus surveillance on the movement of mutants in the Swamp. Here, these are special surveillance devices.
Alright. But what are these devices for? Some kind of biolocator?
I don't know. He just said to pass them along and that he'll explain later why they're needed.
I see, that's just like him. Instead of a good weapon or sturdy armor, he sends this.
Well, take care then.
Do you have any work for me?

Here's the deal. Despite being a neutral territory, Duty plays a crucial role in the Bar. The command has issued an order to strengthen the positions and ensure overall security, which I help with my area of expertise, radio-electronic warfare. A large number of wanderers of all sorts from the Zone gather here, various deals and negotiations take place, different methods and communication channels are used. Eavesdropping on potential competitors or victims is one of the favorite tactics of local mercenaries. We have a very serious problem that may be related to this. Several groups of stalkers have recently gone missing in the Bar area. The situation has Mercenaries and Bandits written all over it. We need to find out who exactly is behind these disappearances.
I'm willing to assist Duty in this investigation.
Not right now.
No, I won't take on this task.

Great. We already have some ideas on how to tackle this, we're going to approach it in a non-standard way. Go and see the radio operators, take the short training course on working with radio electronics. After that, we'll try to identify the extremists through their radio communications. Also, here, take the money for the training course.
Nitro sent me. He said you would teach me the necessary skills in radio-electronic warfare.

Yeah, I know. Just be aware that you'll have to undergo gradual training, and it's not free. I'll provide you with specific information on radio-electronic warfare, from basic to advanced levels. You'll be able to apply the skills of radio-electronic warfare in practice this way, setting up frequency jammers, bypassing them and whatnot.
I'm ready to learn the necessary skills.
I'll take advantage of this opportunity later.
I don't have enough money right now. Strange, Nitro didn't warn me that it would cost money.
I have completed the training course. I had to spend quite a sum though.
Well, those radio operators are quite cunning. Despite my recommendations they didn't want to do it for free, since you're not with Duty. By the way, here's your reward.
What should I do now?
We'll decide the best course of action. Wait while I gather the necessary materials for now.
So, have you decided yet?
You'll have to help me conduct radio reconnaissance by performing various measurements using a special device - an oscilloscope. This might allow us to find out how these groups are operating.
Okay. I didn't expect that the war against extremists would come down to electronic warfare, but not everyone fights in open fields and between anomalies I guess.

We've been fighting on two fronts from the start. Now listen, we'll have to jam the frequencies of Mercenaries and other illegal formations while also protecting our own communication channels. For that, we'll use modulation and signal transformation. You will be working on the first front. Check the old abandoned hangar. Our guys from the combat squads recently searched it and stumbled upon a gathering of radio equipment. Someone might be using it for their purposes. Here's the oscilloscope, it's not too hard to use. Just test the equipment frequencies if there are any, and bring me the results. We'll think about the next steps based on those readings.
I managed to gather information from the radios.
Any problems?
Yes. I was attacked by a group of mercenaries. They were most likely warned about our plan.

I'll keep that in mind, they couldn't have deduced anything from the information provided by Barkeep though. We'll continue once the data you obtained is processed and decide what to do next then, I'll also inform my superiors. That said, we've already obtained some information. We need to start working with the Clear Sky faction, because there's a possibility that this provocation was prepared by agents hiding among them. Go see Nimble, the leader of the Clear Sky group. He is their key coordinator, but most importantly, he is loyal to Duty. You'll be working with him.
Nitro sent me. We're investigating the extremist groups' attacks on stalkers in the Bar. According to the information we've obtained, the threads lead to your people.

Yes, I'm aware. I've already informed Nitro that I'll help you in any way I can. Here, take this special radio. Here's the plan: We need to narrow down the search area. The mole is either among the southern squads who are currently at Garbage, or somewhere else nearby.
Alright, where do I need to establish contact and what exactly do I transmit over the air?
First, head to Misty Forest, then to the tallest tower in the Bar. The radio station is already set up, all you need to do is test all five channels we use for communication.
Understood. I'll do it.
I've transmitted the false signal at the locations you said, here's the radio. What do I do now?
Now we wait for the results. Nitro and the radio operators have already joined the operation. However, you need to prepare for our next step right away.
What exactly do I need to do? And who do you think is most likely involved in this mess - your southern squads?

Most likely, but anything is possible. For now, we need special listening devices, they're like personal bugs. Once we identify the squad, we'll need to pinpoint the specific agent. Go to the Duty supplier for the necessary supplies.
Okay, I'll go see the supplier.
Hey there, Nimble sent me. He said you'll give me everything I need to create the special listening devices.
Yes, I know. I've already prepared everything you need. Here, take it and sign off. It's all in perfect condition. Go to Nitro - he'll give you a soldering station.
Thank you.

So, it seems like Nimble and I have reached an agreement about the job. I need to prepare individual bugs to identify the mole among the Clear Sky faction. I need the soldering station - the supplier said I should get it from you.

Yes, of course. Here you go. Once you've crafted the bugs, report back to Nimble and give the soldering station to him. We should have fully decrypted the intercepted signals by then. After that we'll know for sure whether to search Garbage or here for the spies.
Got it.
Alright, I've crafted the bugs. Here's the soldering station. What's next?
Excellent. We managed to pinpoint the exact source of the threat.
Where is it?

As we suspected, the southeastern Mercenary brigades and several Bandit groups in Garbage are involved. Their informant is among our people at the flea market. You need to go to Garbage fast, identify and then eliminate them.
Understood. I'm heading to Garbage.
Well, my friend, I managed to complete the task. I was able to identify the mole among the Clear Sky members. He led me to the extremist group, which has now been eliminated.
Excellent work. That Clear Sky member has also been taken care of. Nimble also thanks you for your assistance.
How much am I owed, in monetary terms?
Here's your share. You did a great job, my friend. If you ever need anything don't hesitate to ask. I believe we can continue working together in the future.
Certainly. Thank you.
Tell me about the rules of the Arena.
We have six types of battles here: Duel, Hunt, Bodyguard, Partner, Survival, and Avenger. Which type would you like to know more about?
About Duel...
About Hunt...
About Bodyguard...
About Partner...
About Survival...
About Avenger...

In this battle you go one-on-one with an opponent. I'll provide you with the necessary gear for the fight, and then it's off to the Arena. The one who survives and leaves their opponent as a lifeless sack wins. With each new stage, I'll match you against stronger opponents. On the fifth and final stage, you'll compete for the title of Arena Master in the Duel class. And remember, the door to the Arena remains electrified until the end of the duel.

In this battle you face mutants on your own. I'll equip you with sufficient gear for a successful hunt. With each new stage, I'll select stronger monsters for you to hunt. On the fifth and stage, you'll compete for the title of Arena Master in the Hunt class. And remember, the door to the Arena remains electrified until the end of the duel.

In this battle your task is to protect your unarmed partner. Grab a weapon and armor and get to work. To win, you must kill all the attackers while ensuring your partner survives. If both your partner and the attackers end up dead, you won't receive a reward, and it's unlikely you'll continue fighting in this format. With each new stage, the attackers become stronger and more numerous. On the fifth and final stage, you'll compete for the title of Arena Master in the Bodyguard class. And remember, the door to the Arena remains electrified until the end of the duel.

In this battle your task is to destroy the enemy squad together with your partner. Grab your weapon and get into the fight. To win, none of the enemy squad members should survive. On the other hand, your partner must stay alive too. If he dies, you won't receive a reward for the battle and won't be able to continue fighting in this format. With each new stage, the enemy squad becomes stronger and larger. On the fifth and final stage, you'll compete for the title of Arena Master in the Partner class. And remember, the door to the Arena remains electrified until the end of the duel.

In this battle you'll face mutants on your own. I'll provide you with minimal gear. Your task is to survive for a certain period of time. You can shoot at the mutants of course, but you're unlikely to have enough bullets for all of them, and it may attract even more. With each new stage, I'll select stronger monsters for you to face. On the fifth and final stage, you'll compete for the title of Arena Master in the Survival class. And remember, the door to the Arena remains electrified until the end of the duel.

In this battle your task is to single-handedly destroy the enemy squad. You have enough weapons and ammunition. To win, none of the enemy squad members should survive. With each new stage the enemy squad becomes stronger and larger. On the fifth and final stage, you'll compete for the title of Arena Master in the Avenger class. And remember, the door remains electrified until the end of the duel.
Interesting.
Cool.
Who came up with all that?
Oh, come on.
Nice.
Is that so?
Did you handle it? Well done! Hand over all the gear.
Here's everything I have.
Your personal gear is in the safe in the corridor, and Kalgan has your reward.
Your gear, as usual, is in the safe.
Alright.
Understood, thank you.
I'm here for my reward for winning at the Arena.
I've already given everything I owe you.
Here's the reward for Avenger Stage 1.
Here's the reward for Avenger Stage 2.
Here's the reward for Avenger Stage 3.
Here's the reward for Avenger Stage 4.
Here's the reward for Avenger Stage 5.
Here's the reward for Bodyguard Stage 1.
Here's the reward for Bodyguard Stage 2.
Here's the reward for Bodyguard Stage 3.
Here's the reward for Bodyguard Stage 4.
Here's the reward for Bodyguard Stage 5.
Here's the reward for Duel Stage 1.
Here's the reward for Duel Stage 2.
Here's the reward for Duel Stage 3.
Here's the reward for Duel Stage 4.
Here's the reward for Duel Stage 5.
Here's the reward for Partner Stage 1.
Here's the reward for Partner Stage 2.
Here's the reward for Partner Stage 3.
Here's the reward for Partner Stage 4.
Here's the reward for Partner Stage 5.
Here's the reward for Hunt Stage 1.
Here's the reward for Hunt Stage 2.
Here's the reward for Hunt Stage 3.
Here's the reward for Hunt Stage 4.
Here's the reward for Hunt Stage 5.
Here's the reward for Survival Stage 1.
Here's the reward for Survival Stage 2.
Here's the reward for Survival Stage 3.
Here's the reward for Survival Stage 4.
Here's the reward for Survival Stage 5.
Thank you.
Do you participate in Arena fights?
Yeah, for sure.
Well, you're a cool dude. Come by when you're really ready.
Tell me about your specialization.
I also want to participate in Arena fights. Do you want to join me in a Bodyguard fight?

I'm Vasya Rod from the Burnt Brigade. I decided to make some quick cash at the Arena. It's kind of scary, but Kalgan promised good money for protecting clients. I don't have decent armor, but I'll back you up like a fly on shit. Those clowns won't get a chance.
No problem, as long as you don't screw up.
Understood.
Yeah, fine by me.
Not yet, I'll think about it.
Alright. Let's kick some ass at the Arena.
See you at the Arena.
Do you participate in Arena fights?
Yes.
I'm always here if you change your mind.
Tell me about your specialization.
I also want to participate in Arena fights. Do you want to join me in a Bodyguard fight?

I'm Petr Bolotnik from the Swamps, as you can guess. I used to be with Clear Sky, but now I'm working in the Arena. I have decent armor and a helmet, but they restrict my movement a bit, so keep a close eye on me.
I'm fine with it if it suits you.
Understood.
Yeah, everything's fine by me.
Not yet, I'll think about it.
Alright. See you at the Arena.
See you.
Do you participate in Arena fights?
As Monolith is my witness - yes.
I'm always here if not in prayer.
Tell me about your specialization.
I also want to participate in Arena fights. Do you want to join me in a Bodyguard fight?

I'm Innocent Frostbitten, a former follower of great Monolith. Nowadays I do Bodyguard fights at the Arena. Praise Monolith I have powerful armor, but it's also quite bulky, so you need to wait for me to catch up.
If it's the will of Monolith, I don't object.
Understood.
Alright then.
Not yet, I'll think about it.
That's good. May Monolith protect you.
See you at the Arena.
How was the last fight in the Arena?
We haven't fought yet.
You knocked them down nicely.
Not bad, but they almost got you.
Listen buddy, if things keep going like this, we'll be wearing Versace sneakers.
Imagine if I had stumbled, damn, keep an eye on those idiots.
Wow, buddy, you smashed them. I've got some serious cash.
It's alright.
Yeah, everything went well, I guess.
Not bad, not bad. A couple more times and I'll have enough for a cottage in the village.
Phew, I was on the brink of death, thank God it all worked out.
Great, you took care of them. Now I've made some decent money and don't have to take risks like that.
Everything's fine.
Awesome, everything is going really well.
Everything is perfect, praise Monolith.
It's fine, just a little damage to the armor.
Everything's great, I'm alive and well, and the armor is intact. Praise Monolith.
Understood.
Do you participate in the Arena fights?
Yeah, brother.
Anyway, I'm here if anything.
Tell me about your specialization.
I want to participate in the Arena fights. Do you want to join as my partner?
I'm Gena Shovel from the Sopatogo Brigade. I just love brawling alongside a partner you know? My armor is weak, but I've got a solid gun.
No problem, I can bash someone's skull in right now.
Understood.
Okay then.
No, wait, I'll think about it some more.
Bye for now. See you on the battlefield.
See you at the Arena.
Do you participate in the Arena fights?
Yes.
Understood. I'll wait if you change your mind.
Tell me about your specialization.
I want to participate in the Arena fights. Do you want to join as my partner?
I'm Kolya Lucky from the Free Stalkers. I earn my bread with fights alongside a partner. As you can see, my jacket is not super, but I have some decent weapons.
I'm okay with it if it suits you.
Understood.
Yeah, everything suits me.
No, wait, I'll think about it some more.
Alright. See you at the Arena.
See you.
Do you participate in the Arena fights?
Affirmative.
Come by later if you change your mind.
Tell me about your specialization.
I want to participate in the Arena fights. Do you want to join as my partner?

I'm Mitya Brace, a former soldier, now a deserter and a free shooter. I'm up for earning some cash in a fight with a competent partner. My armor is lightweight military gear, but I only have a simple pistol for weapons.
I'm in if it suits you.
Understood.
Yeah, everything suits me.
No, wait, I'll think about it some more.
Alright. See you at the Arena.
See you at the Arena.
How was the last fight at the Arena?
We haven't fought yet.
Awesome, but I didn't warm up enough.
Great, I was just blasting away with my gun.
We simply tore them apart, brother.
Man, it was tough.
Brother, we're like two terminators.
Alright.
Good, we're managing well together.
Wonderful, we crushed them.
Not bad, but I couldn't help looking behind.
That's it, we're champions. Now we'll meet all the babes.
Not bad, you move skillfully and provide good cover.
Getting better and better, we'll definitely make a great team.
Excellent, with you, it's like a brave special forces unit, we crushed everyone.
It was tough, but we didn't miss a shot, took down the enemies.
Great, we're champion brother.
Understood.
I need medical assistance.
No problem, let me take a look...
[continue...]
Are you kidding? You're very healthy!
Hello my friend, what brings you here? How's life treating you?
I want to sign up for a fight.
What type of fight do you want to sign up for?
Whatever you say.
I want to sign up for Duel.
I want to sign up for Hunt.
I want to sign up for Bodyguard.
I want to sign up for Partner.
I want to sign up for Survival.
I want to sign up for Avenger.
No, I don't think I'll risk losing my life.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 1 of the Duel.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 2 of the Duel.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 3 of the Duel.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 4 of the Duel.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 5 of the Duel.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 1 of the Hunt.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 2 of the Hunt.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 3 of the Hunt.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 4 of the Hunt.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 5 of the Hunt.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 1 of the Bodyguard.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 2 of the Bodyguard.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 3 of the Bodyguard.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 4 of the Bodyguard.
All right, I'm signing you up for Stage 5 of the Bodyguard.
For this fight, you need a partner whom you will guard. Make a deal with one of the stalkers, they usually hang out in the tunnel near the fans.
I already told you, make a deal with one of the stalkers to participate in the fight.
Unfortunately your previous partner died, no one wants to associate with you.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 1 of the Partner fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 2 of the Partner fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 3 of the Partner fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 4 of the Partner fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 5 of the Partner fight.
For this fight, you need a partner. Make a deal with one of the stalkers, they usually hang out in the tunnel near the fans.
I already told you, make a deal with one of the stalkers to participate in this fight.
Unfortunately your previous partner died, no one wants to associate with you.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 1 of the Survival fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 2 of the Survival fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 3 of the Survival fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 4 of the Survival fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 5 of the Survival fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 1 of the Avenger fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 2 of the Avenger fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 3 of the Avenger fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 4 of the Avenger fight.
Okay, I'm signing you up for Stage 5 of the Avenger fight.
Agreed.
Personal belongings go in the safe.
Okay.
Here's the equipment for the fight.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Let's go.
Good luck!
I see the Arena is up and running again?
Yes and not only that, we have expanded and now have several types of fights on the Arena, with five stages each.
Great, I would like to participate in the fights.

No problem, you can learn the details and rules from my assistant - Crusher. You can also sign up for fights by talking to him, he handles the equipment too. You will receive your rewards for winning fights from me.
Got it, we'll meet again then.
There you go, now you're all patched up. You'll live.
Phew, we barely made it! Hello, are you the one in charge here?
Quite an emission today, I don't like this at all... Who are you?

I'm Shroud. My friend and I made it to Garbage through an underground laboratory from Cordon, following Sidorovich's lead. There were some heavily armed people there trying to kill us. They gassed us, we barely survived.
I see, Sidorovich informed me about you. Rest up for now, recover, we'll talk after the emission is over. I'll have a job for you then.
Alright.
We found some documents in the laboratory under Cordon. It seems to be fragmentary information about what was happening there. Sidorovich asked me to give them to you.
Show me what you have.
Document-1
Document-2
Document-3
I'll look through them in my spare time. If there's anything useful, I'll let you know. Oh, I almost forgot, here's something for your trouble.
That's all I have, thank you.
What's the matter? Tell me.

I already know from Sidorovich that you're interested in finding the Pendulum artifact and that you witnessed the damned chaos surrounding it. People are ready to tear each other apart for that one... So, just recently I received a lot of interesting information regarding this whole affair. It seems like you guys are reliable and can be trusted. Interested?
Of course. Go on.

Here's the deal... An unknown but very wealthy artifact collector has announced an incredible reward for whoever finds Pendulum. It's known that a private military company called "Spectrum" organized an expedition to search for it in the Zone, but they can't seem to find it, lacking the necessary information. I don't know where these rumors come from, but some claim that the Monolith faction still guards this artifact and that it's stored somewhere underground in the Zone. Everyone's gone crazy over the money offered for its discovery... Bandits, Mercenaries, Stalkers, loners, they all roam underground areas and dive into anomalies just to find this mysterious Pendulum.
Do you have any information about where it might be?

All of this is very strange, there hasn't been such a frenzy since Strelok deactivated the Brain Scorcher and everyone rushed to the center. New stalkers are entering the Zone, and retired experienced ones are returning from far away. But enough about that, yes, I have some information. Sidorovich vouched for you, so I think we can work together.
I'll discuss your proposal with my partner.
Alright, I'll give you a couple of days to think it over. Just be careful not to take too long, I have other interested parties in mind.
See you soon.
Oh, you're up.

How are you? We got beat up pretty badly huh? I thought it was all over... I even started recalling my prayers. Anyway, enough sentimentality. Barkeep here is offering us a good job, would you be interested?
Yeah... Man, my head is killing me... I can't shake off the effects of poisoning. So, what's he offering?
He says he has information about the possible location of the Pendulum artifact. I feel like it won't be as easy as it sounds, but I still want to give it a try and find it.

Everyone at the Bar is talking about that artifact. They say people are searching all over the Zone for it. So many have disappeared... some shot by Bandits, others lost in anomalies. There are whispers about some group called "Spectrum" as well. I've had enough adventures and fed up. Plus, I don't like this whole story about an unbelievably large reward. I think I'll try to return to the outside world once I regain my senses. That's my final decision. Sorry brother.
So you're not with me?

No, no, no - don't even try to persuade me. I've had enough of this dealing with Sidor, now I'll be smarter. I don't recommend you to get involved either. Barkeep is a real schemer, he's already sent so many people to their doom with his tasks. Do you know why he chose you? Because he doesn't care who he sends to those deadly places, just to confirm his guesses.
Well, I'll still try my luck. Farewell, my friend!
Here I am.
What's your decision?
I'm ready to cooperate.
Great, where's your buddy?
He decided not to join me.

Well, no problem, your share will be bigger that way. So, here's the deal: The Duty faction captured one of Logopedist's lieutenants, a Bandit boss in Cordon. You've probably crossed paths with him... Your task is to go and speak with Duty's leader, Voronin. They extracted some information from the Bandit during the interrogation before they took him out.
Is that all? I just have to go and talk with him?
Yes, that's it for now. Go and take care of that. I need to clarify a few things through my channels and cross-reference the information from your documents.
Wouldn't it have been easier for you to find out everything from him yourself?
When did you get so smart? If I say go and talk, then that's what needs to be done. Come on now, get moving.
Okay, I'm going.
Hello! Barkeep sent me.
So, you're the guy... the one who stirred up all this commotion? I'll give you the information we managed to extract from that scumbag, but I'll need you to do a little job for me in return. Agreed?
Agreed.

So, we spotted our Bandit friend when he crawled out of some hole and tried to break through the Duty base to get outside. We had a polite conversation with him and learned the following: You know that Monolith member who disguised himself as a Freedomer? Turns out he scoured the entire neighborhood. He was seen multiple times, even lingered here for quite a while, supposedly came far away using secret tunnels. Apparently, there's an underground complex beneath the Rostok factory, where we are right now. From there, a secret underground railway leads straight to the closed city of Kurchatov, that's where he came from. Haven't seen anyone who's been there even once, there are ominous rumors about it. Anyway, the Bandit didn't know what's happening inside the city, he turned out to be rather dense. To get into these tunnels, you need to get past the locked metal door here in our base. We keep it under constant surveillance... just in case.
That's informative. What do I have to do in return for this information?

No big deal! You just need to stand guard with my guys at the checkpoint. Recon reported that mutants have multiplied to an absurd extent in Wild Territory. They might attack the Bar, most likely very soon too. Stand guard there for a day, then you're free.
Alright.
I'll let you know when your shift starts. Don't wander too far from the Bar.
Deal.

Hey, listen carefully. You'll be taking up your position here and stand with my soldiers for a day. I'm increasing the manpower at the checkpoint today, because we're expecting an onslaught of mutants from Wild Territory. Unauthorized leaving of the post is considered desertion and is punishable under wartime laws. Understood?
Yes, I'll start my duties.
Well, hero, our guys got your back. You took a little nap but now you're as cool as a cucumber! It was a bit heavy huh?
A bit, you say?! My ears is still ringing and my left eye keeps twitching.
Calm down man, the main thing is that you're alive. You've fulfilled your duty and are free to go now.
I'm leaving.
Well... What's up...
I saw how they dragged you in here. I thought we'd meet on the other side. But you're as tough as a dog! So, tell me... what did you learn from Voronin?

Yeah, got a little roughed up, not the first time. Anyway, he says there are underground tunnels under the Bar. Some kind of laboratory or something... it's sealed with a locked door. Apparently, my "Monolith guy" came out of there recently. Have you heard of Kurchatov?

Hmm... Interesting coincidence. I've actually been digging up information about that closed city recently. I sent a group of stalkers there for reconnaissance not long ago. They went through the Army Warehouses and haven't been in touch for a week. A few days ago, one guy barely made it back, saying that all hell is breaking loose there! Mutants and zombies are rampant... a real nightmare. I think it would be safer for you to reach Kurchatov through the catacombs.
Are you sure it's possible to reach the city that way?

I have some maps showing the underground sections leading to Kurchatov. Some details were missing since mice chewed up the exact place where the underground descent points were marked. Based on Voronin's information, these tunnels could connect the Bar with Kurchatov.
But how do we get there? Voronin said the passage is sealed?
Let me think... Hmm... You said it's sealed right? I have some ideas...
Spill the beans already.

We have a small acid swamp nearby, and there's a house there where some clearly deranged guy is settled. I don't have reliable information about him, who he is or where he's from, and I don't really care. People say he used to work in the Consciousness laboratories, doing experiments on humans, they say that's what drove him mad. I don't believe it myself. Go see him, maybe he knows how to get into the laboratory. And... be on your guard.
Alright, I'll go. I'll try to find out something.
Hello my dear friend! Long time no see. I suppose you've been busy with work... How's the wife?
What?! Are you out of your mind?
Hold on now, Senka, don't confuse me!
I'm not Senka, uncle. I'm here on business. I need the key to the laboratory. Can you help me out?
Shh... keep it down, it's always winter there, turn off the light. I can give you the key. I bet you've been looking into the hospital.
Hey, look at me, concentrate. The key to the entrance door. The key. To the laboratory door. Where is it?
Ha, pencil, sharpener, swamp – artifact! Trade. Paid for with state funds, I swear.
Pfft... will you start making sense?
Artifact! Artifacts!!! Everything shifts, I can't concentrate. It's all like a dream. I'm not dreaming, am I?
No, you're not dreaming. Do you need artifacts? I'll trade them for the key if you want?
Yes... no, yes, yes. I'll trade. And the key is golden too! All life on Earth springs from it...
Man, you're out of your... Well, you need artifacts? I'll gather everything here in this swamp. After that you'll give me the key though, otherwise I'll take it myself.
Yeees, yes! Trade... (continues mumbling something inaudible)
Wait here.
Hello again.
Why are you covered in mud? Ugh... and you stink too. When was the last time you took a shower?
No no, it's because of the swamp. The one near the crazy old man's house... may he rest in peace.
Rest in peace? Did you kill him or something?
It wasn't intentional, I had to! He was out of his mind... babbling about something. But never mind what he said. Long story short, I have the key to that door. Why did you call me?

Alright, since you have the key, it means you'll soon go to that laboratory. I don't know what or who awaits you there. I'm very interested in you surviving and coming back here with the loot though, so I'm giving you a gun and a bulletproof vest. Just don't even think about running away and selling them! They're rare... If they show up in the inventory of any trader I'll be the first to know, and you'll have to answer for it. Understand?
Yeah, I'm not a fool. I'm off.
Hey there!
Did Hoarse sent you?
Who is Hoarse?
Alright, forget it. I'm waiting for a guy here. Been waiting for a while now... Maybe you've heard anything about him?
No, I haven't heard anything. Who are you and what are you doing here?
Who's asking?
I'm Shroud. Just passing by, seeing some friendly faces, thought I'd ask who you are. Maybe I can get a job or something.
I'm not the employment agency. Go talk to Barkeep; he's always happy to give people something to do.
Well, thanks, but I've already talked to him. Seems like you're not in the mood for conversation.
How can you tell? Listen, ever heard of the Clear Sky faction? We're all that's left of it.
We've crossed paths, but I don't know much about you guys.
Are you new around here or something?
Well, you could say that... I'm not exactly a veteran, but I've tasted gunpowder and felt anomalies on my backside.

I see. So, our faction was settled in the Swamps. We were involved in various activities. Our eggheads mostly studied artifacts, while we provided gear and security for the whole operation. Everyone was doing fine until we got the crazy idea to go to CNPP. It's a long story, maybe someday I'll write a book about it and you can read it. Anyway, we got hit by a massive emission there. Most of us didn't expect such a turn of events and died on the spot. I had enough time to hide in a shelter. When I came out, all I saw were corpses. They say someone even made a game about it on the outside world. Maybe someone from our survivors... I don't know.
Yeah, man. The Zone got to you. So, what are your plans now? Who are you waiting for?

Us Clear Sky survivors have gathered here. We're thinking of revisiting our old stomping grounds. The places where Lebedev and Suslov used to hide artifacts. It was an interesting thing they did, studying the artifacts and then returning them to the Zone, I don't know why. So that's it, we're preparing to go to that area.
So, you're planning to search for artifacts?

Of course, I'm tired of digging around in the damp and dirt here. I'm freezing my ass off and my knees have been aching for the past two years. Think about it, why not give it a try when the money is very good? We're waiting for Hoarse, if you happen to meet him, tell him that. He's been off the radar for a couple of days now.
I'll pass on the message if I see him. Take care.
You too, stay safe.
